creator: oberman@es.net
text:
Daniel,

I must apologize for not getting back to you promptly, but I have been
on vacation and off-line. Just got back and saw your message (buried in
about 1000). I was hoping to have time to read the updated handbook entry
before responding, so I did not send an immediate response on Thursday.

I any case, closing the ticket was correct as the current handbook
discussion odes match the man page and is far clearer than te page at
the time I submitted the PR.

Again, sorry for the lack of a prompt response and thanks for your work
on the handbook.
-- 
R. Kevin Oberman, Network Engineer
Energy Sciences Network (ESnet)
Ernest O. Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (Berkeley Lab)
E-mail: oberman@es.net			Phone: +1 510 486-8634
Key fingerprint:059B 2DDF 031C 9BA3 14A4  EADA 927D EBB3 987B 3751

> Date: Sat, 9 Sep 2006 00:04:26 +0200
> From: Daniel Gerzo <danger@FreeBSD.org>
> 
> Hello Kevin,
> 
>   Could you please tell me, which man page have you been referring to?
>   Note that I have committed a small update to the IPFW chapter of our
>   Handbook, which I hope describes the differences between
>   firewall_type and firewall_script directives well. Please check
>   http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/handbook/firewalls-ipfw.html
> 
>   Let me know if there are any further problems which need to be
>   resolved. If no feedback will be received, I will close this PR in
>   a week.
> 
> -- 
> Best regards,
>  Daniel                          mailto:danger@FreeBSD.org
>
